codeReview.summary: The hook now matches the feature contract: `src/hooks/use-auto-lock.ts` removes the timeout dependency, only reacts on `active -> background|inactive` edges, skips already-locked sessions, and calls both `session.lock()` and `agentStore.teardown()` (`src/hooks/use-auto-lock.ts:44-65`). The added hook tests cover the exact call-count requirements (`src/hooks/__tests__/use-auto-lock.test.tsx:103-204`), and the existing cross-area auto-lock flow still verifies that the next explicit unlock re-prompts biometrics after teardown (`src/__tests__/cross-area/auto-lock-flow.test.tsx:193-270`). I did not find a high-confidence correctness bug in the committed implementation.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker marked `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true`, but the transcript skeleton does not show the required library-doc reads or a red test run before implementation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/mobile-feature-worker/SKILL.md` requires reading `.factory/library/architecture.md` at minimum, plus `.factory/library/environment.md` when running test flows, and requires observing tests fail before implementation. The transcript skeleton for session `1fd71b1c-2bd8-4a08-bd9a-e3acc0557bcc` (`worker-transcripts.jsonl:35`) shows reads for `mission.md`, `validation-contract.md`, `AGENTS.md`, source files, and `.factory/services.yaml`, then creates `src/hooks/__tests__/use-auto-lock.test.tsx` and `src/hooks/use-auto-lock.ts` before running the targeted Jest command. The handoff still reports `followedProcedure: true` with no deviations (`handoffs/2026-04-22T21-46-11-999Z__auto-lock-hook-teardown__1fd71b1c-2bd8-4a08-bd9a-e3acc0557bcc.json:80-81`).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The validation contract's auto-lock evidence has drifted out of sync with the actual test suite, so its prescribed `bunx jest ... -t ...` commands no longer point at real evidence.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `validation-contract.md:1710` expects `src/hooks/__tests__/use-auto-lock.test.tsx -t "tears down agent on background and inactive"`, but the committed file uses separate cases instead (`src/hooks/__tests__/use-auto-lock.test.tsx:103,113,123,147,164,175`). `validation-contract.md:1732` expects `src/hooks/__tests__/use-auto-lock.e2e.test.tsx -t "requires fresh biometric after teardown"`, but no such file exists under `src/hooks/`; the fresh-biometric behavior is currently covered in `src/__tests__/cross-area/auto-lock-flow.test.tsx:193-270`.

summary: Review passed. The auto-lock hook implementation and tests satisfy the feature's requested behavior, and I found no blocking code issues. I recorded two shared-state gaps: the worker's handoff overstates procedure compliance, and the validation contract's auto-lock test pointers are stale.
